rows.criterion: Abandoned SPLITTING record auto-reverts after lease timeout
Subsequent BeginSplit for same fileID succeeds after reclaim
Lease timeout uses an injectable/mockable clock, not real sleep
Pre-existing double-split protection (immediate re-BeginSplit still refused) is not weakened
rows.test: TestAbandonedSplittingRecoversAfterTimeout (assertion before advancing the clock) + pre-existing TestSplittingStatusIsolation

rows.criterion: Reclaimed split behaves like a normal one afterward (EndSplit succeeds, guard released)
NewOrchestrator remains backward compatible for existing 3-arg call sites
rows.test: go build ./engine/split/... (all pre-existing call sites in execute_test.go, orchestrate_test.go compile unchanged)

rows.criterion: No regression in rest of engine/split package (excluding concurrently-edited split_race_test.go)
rows.test: go test ./engine/split/... -race -run '<all test names except TestConcurrentAppendSplitRace>'

excluded_from_this_run.reason: File has uncommitted, in-progress edits from a concurrent agent (subtask 4.5.3.6). Test timed out under -race during this run; not attributable to this subtask's changes (orchestrate.go/orchestrate_test.go only), per explicit scope-isolation instruction not to let it block this run. Not verified pass/fail here by design -- left to the owning agent/verification pass for that file.
